title: Vector2D Object
description: Transient 2D vector. This object is a wrapper for 2D vector data and is used to pass vector data in and out of the API. They are created statically using the create method of the Vector2D class.

methods.name: add
methods.description: Add a vector to this vector.

methods.name: angleTo
methods.description: Gets the angle between this vector and another vector.

methods.name: asArray
methods.description: Returns the vector values as an array [x, y].

methods.name: asPoint
methods.description: Return a point with the same x and y values as this vector.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: copy
methods.description: Creates and returns an independent copy of this Vector2D object.

methods.name: dotProduct
methods.description: Calculates the Dot Product of this vector and an input vector.

methods.name: isEqualTo
methods.description: Compare this vector with another to check for equality.

methods.name: isParallelTo
methods.description: Compare this vector with another to check for parallelism.

methods.name: isPerpendicularTo
methods.description: Compare this vector with another to check for perpendicularity.

methods.name: normalize
methods.description: Normalizes the vector. Normalization makes the vector length equal to one. The vector should not be zero length.

methods.name: scaleBy
methods.description: Scales the vector by specifying a scaling factor.

methods.name: setWithArray
methods.description: Sets the definition of the vector by specifying an array containing the x and y coordinates.

methods.name: subtract
methods.description: Subtract a vector from this vector.

methods.name: transformBy
methods.description: Transforms the vector by specifying a 2D transformation matrix.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: length
properties.description: Gets the length of the vector.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

properties.name: x
properties.description: Gets and sets the X coordinate of the vector.

properties.name: y
properties.description: Gets and sets the Y coordinate of the vector.

add.title: Vector2D.add Method
add.description: Add a vector to this vector.

parameters.name: vector
parameters.description: The vector to add to this vector.

angleTo.title: Vector2D.angleTo Method
angleTo.description: Gets the angle between this vector and another vector.

parameters.name: vector
parameters.description: The vector to measure the angle to.

asArray.title: Vector2D.asArray Method
asArray.description: Returns the vector values as an array [x, y].
asArray.return_description: Returns an array of the vector's values [x, y].

asPoint.title: Vector2D.asPoint Method
asPoint.description: Return a point with the same x and y values as this vector.

classType.title: Vector2D.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

copy.title: Vector2D.copy Method
copy.description: Creates and returns an independent copy of this Vector2D object.
copy.return_description: Returns a new Vector2D object that is a copy of this Vector2D object.

create.title: Vector2D.create Method

syntax.python:
# Uses no optional arguments.
returnValue = adsk.core.Vector2D.create()

# Uses optional arguments.
returnValue = adsk.core.Vector2D.create(x, y)

create.return_description: Returns the new Vector2D object or null if the creation failed.

parameters.name: x
parameters.description: The x coordinate of the vector.   This is an optional argument whose default value is 0.0.

parameters.name: y
parameters.description: The y coordinate of the vector.   This is an optional argument whose default value is 0.0.

dotProduct.title: Vector2D.dotProduct Method
dotProduct.description: Calculates the Dot Product of this vector and an input vector.
dotProduct.return_description: Returns the dot product of the two vectors.

parameters.name: vector
parameters.description: The vector to use in the dot product calculation.

isEqualTo.title: Vector2D.isEqualTo Method
isEqualTo.description: Compare this vector with another to check for equality.
isEqualTo.return_description: Returns true if the vectors are equal.

parameters.name: vector
parameters.description: The vector to compare with for equality.

related_samples.name: Create Setups From Hole Recognition API Sample
related_samples.description: This sample script demonstrates how to create a correctly oriented setup using Hole Recognition functionality.The Fusion Manufacturing Extension is required for Hole Recognition.The script starts by opening a sample model from the CAM Samples folder via its URN. The model comprises a 3 way coupling containing holes in various orientations and mounted on a fixture. A reference setup is created for the model using a simple stock mode and offsets. The Hole Recognition feature of the Fusion Manufacturing Extension creates 5 hole groups containing 11 holes between them. For each unique hole group vector captured, a new setup is created and its orientation transformed to match the vector.

isParallelTo.title: Vector2D.isParallelTo Method
isParallelTo.description: Compare this vector with another to check for parallelism.
isParallelTo.return_description: Returns true if the vectors are parallel.

parameters.name: vector
parameters.description: The vector to compare with for parallelism.

isPerpendicularTo.title: Vector2D.isPerpendicularTo Method
isPerpendicularTo.description: Compare this vector with another to check for perpendicularity.
isPerpendicularTo.return_description: Returns true if the vectors are perpendicular.

parameters.name: vector
parameters.description: The vector to compare with for perpendicularity.

normalize.title: Vector2D.normalize Method
normalize.description: Normalizes the vector. Normalization makes the vector length equal to one. The vector should not be zero length.

scaleBy.title: Vector2D.scaleBy Method
scaleBy.description: Scales the vector by specifying a scaling factor.

parameters.name: scale
parameters.description: The scale factor to multiple the vector by (i.e. 1.5).

setWithArray.title: Vector2D.setWithArray Method
setWithArray.description: Sets the definition of the vector by specifying an array containing the x and y coordinates.

parameters.name: coordinates
parameters.description: An array that specifies the values for the x and y coordinates of the vector.

subtract.title: Vector2D.subtract Method
subtract.description: Subtract a vector from this vector.

parameters.name: vector
parameters.description: The vector to subtract from this vector.

transformBy.title: Vector2D.transformBy Method
transformBy.description: Transforms the vector by specifying a 2D transformation matrix.

parameters.name: matrix
parameters.description: The Matrix2D object that defines the transformation.

isValid.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = vector2D_var.isValid

length.title: Vector2D.length Property
length.description: Gets the length of the vector.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = vector2D_var.length

objectType.title: Vector2D.objectType Property
objectType.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object. It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.

x.title: Vector2D.x Property
x.description: Gets and sets the X coordinate of the vector.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = vector2D_var.x

# Set the value of the property.
vector2D_var.x = propertyValue

y.title: Vector2D.y Property
y.description: Gets and sets the Y coordinate of the vector.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = vector2D_var.y

# Set the value of the property.
vector2D_var.y = propertyValue
